_meta.author: Claude (national autopilot, federal AL-AR batch)
_meta.note: Federal evidence-finalize batch (5): AL-6 Rep Palmer (R, RSC, Briarwood PCA, House Republican Policy Committee chair 2019-25, primary winner 2026), AL-7 Rep Sewell (D, AL's only Dem House member, Brown Chapel AME Selma, Laken Riley YES crossover Jan 2025, Iran war-powers YES, PP Action Fund endorsed, NARAL 100), AR-1 Rep Crawford (R, House Intelligence Chair Jan 2025, Cross Church / Central Baptist Jonesboro, deviation on FISA 702 + Iran war-powers + Big Ag donor capture but voted NO Ukraine aid), AR-2 Rep Hill (R, House Financial Services Chair Jan 2025, Catholic, former banker, MAJOR Ukraine-aid deviation + J6 commission YES + finance-industry captive but RFMA NO + Pain-Capable cosponsor + FRC 100% + led HFSC ESG WG + leader on CBDC ban HR 5403/HR 1919), AR-3 Rep Womack (R, senior appropriator, Cross Church Pinnacle Hills Rogers SBC, MAJOR Ukraine-aid deviation + Yemen war-powers NAY + Tyson/GIPSA Big Ag capture + AIPAC $134k + McCarthy-keep institutionalist). All five primary-won/uncontested for 2026 general. Mike Rogers AL-3 still DROPPED for slug-collision w/ MD-32 Delegate Mike Rogers (D) per prior batch.

profile.religion: Presbyterian (PCA) — longtime member, Briarwood Presbyterian Church, Birmingham AL
profile.background: U.S. Representative AL-6 (R), in office since Jan 2015. Chaired House Republican Policy Committee 2019-2025. Member House Freedom Caucus and Republican Study Committee. Co-founder + president emeritus, Alabama Policy Institute (state-policy think tank, 1989-2014). Univ. of Alabama BS Operations Management. Married to Ann; three children. Defeated Case Dixon 81-19 in May 19, 2026 R primary; advances to Nov 3 2026 general.

gary-palmer.notes_set: U.S. Rep AL-6 (R), in office since 2015; House Republican Policy Committee chair 2019-25; HFC + RSC. [2026-06 federal autopilot] Heritage Action 118th 85% / lifetime ~89%; SBA Pro-Life A+; NRA A; FRC pro-family. Hard-evidence wins: cosponsor Life at Conception Act HR 431; YES Born-Alive HR 26 (2023); NO Respect for Marriage Act HR 8404 (Dec 2022, RC 513) with public opposition press; YES HR 734 Women's Sports Protection (Apr 2023, RC 192); NO Equality Act (HR 5, 117th); cosponsor Educational Choice for Children Act HR 833 (folded into OBBBA July 2025); original cosponsor First Amendment Defense Act HR 2802; cosponsor + YES SAVE Act HR 8281; party-line YES H.R. 2 Secure the Border Act and H.R. 5403 CBDC Anti-Surveillance State Act (both keyed by Heritage Action, Palmer not in named defectors); voted AGAINST FRA 2023 from the right (one of 71 R no-votes citing insufficient cuts); signed AL delegation OSHA-mandate-opposition letter; called Biden vax mandate 'one of the most egregious government overreaches.' No cross-party deviations surfaced in this sweep. Foreign-policy-restraint cells (I-row) largely null — no on-record Iran/Yemen war-powers votes or AIPAC donation total surfaced.

0.note: Cosponsor Life at Conception Act HR 431 (118th) — defines personhood at fertilization; on-record statement on protecting innocent lives.

1.note: YES Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act HR 26 (Jan 2023); cosponsor Life at Conception; SBA Pro-Life A+.

4.note: OpenSecrets shows no PP/NARAL/EMILY's List PAC contributions; Palmer has voted to defund Planned Parenthood (HR 3134).

1.note: NO Respect for Marriage Act HR 8404 (Dec 8, 2022, RC 513); issued public opposition press release.

2.note: YES HR 734 Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act (Apr 2023, RC 192); applauded Trump women's-sports EO.

4.note: NO Equality Act HR 5 (117th) — only 3 R defectors (Reed, Katko, Fitzpatrick); Palmer not among them.

0.note: Cosponsor Educational Choice for Children Act HR 833 (119th); ECCA enacted as part of OBBBA July 4, 2025.

1.note: YES Parents Bill of Rights HR 5 (Mar 24, 2023, RC 161, party-line 213-208 with 5 named R defectors; Palmer not among them).

2.note: YES HR 734 (Title IX/biological-sex women's sports); applauded EO ending DEI agenda.

4.note: Original cosponsor First Amendment Defense Act HR 2802 — protects faith-based actors including adoption agencies from federal penalty.

1.note: Original cosponsor First Amendment Defense Act (conscience protections for religious actors); statement: 'religious rights and other freedoms are not forfeited during a pandemic.'

0.note: Party-line YES on HR 5403 CBDC Anti-Surveillance State Act (May 23, 2024, 216-192); Heritage Action keyed the bill; Palmer not among R defectors. Heritage Action 118th 85%.

2.note: Among 71 House Rs voting NO on FY2023 Fiscal Responsibility Act from fiscal-hawk position; on-record BBA support.

4.note: Party-line YES on 2023 House anti-ESG pension bill (216-204); Palmer not flagged among R defectors.

1.note: Cosponsor + YES on SAVE Act HR 8281 (proof-of-citizenship voter registration, July 2024).

0.note: Party-line YES HR 2 Secure the Border Act (May 11, 2023, 219-213 w/ 2 R defectors; Palmer not among them); on-record border-crisis advocacy.

2.note: Frames border as invasion; HR 2 included sanctuary-city defunding (his YES vote).

0.note: NRA-PVF A grade + NRA-PVF endorsement — HARD evidence of 2A alignment per scorecard convention.

1.note: NRA-PVF A grade reflects opposition to red-flag / mag-limit / AWB legislation.

0.note: Signed AL delegation letter to OSHA opposing federal vax mandate (Nov 2021); called Biden healthcare-worker mandate 'one of the most egregious government overreaches'; cheered SCOTUS rejection of OSHA mandate.

profile.religion: African Methodist Episcopal (AME) — lifelong member, Brown Chapel AME Church, Selma, AL (~30 years; personally donated to its restoration)
profile.background: U.S. Representative AL-7 (D), in office since Jan 2011; Alabama's only Democratic House member. Selma native. Princeton AB, Oxford D.Phil. (Marshall Scholar), Harvard JD. Ranking Member, House Ways and Means subcommittee. Member Congressional Black Caucus, Congressional Equality Caucus. Married; no children publicly noted. Declared candidate Nov 3, 2026 general.

terri-sewell.notes_set: U.S. Rep AL-7 (D), in office since 2011; AL's only Democratic House member; CBC + Equality Caucus. [2026-06 federal autopilot] Heritage Action 119th 0% / lifetime 6%; NRA F; NARAL/Reproductive Freedom for All 100; PP Action Fund 2026 endorsement; EMILY's List backed. Hard-evidence FALSEs (caucus-line D): NO Born-Alive HR 26; cosponsor + YES WHPA HR 3755; YES RFMA HR 8404 (only AL R/D crossover toward marriage equality on the AL delegation); NO HR 734 Women's Sports; YES Equality Act HR 5; NO Parents Bill of Rights HR 5; NO SAVE Act (spoke in opposition on House floor); YES Freedom to Vote: John R. Lewis Act; NO HR 2 Secure the Border Act; YES Dream/Promise Act 2021; YES BSCA 2022 + Bipartisan Background Checks + reinstate-AWB. Three CROSS-PARTY CROSSOVERS (high-value findings, scored TRUE-aligned): (1) G3 mandatory E-Verify — supported as part of Farm Workforce Modernization Act 2021; (2) Laken Riley Act YES on the 2025 final vote (HR 29, Jan 7 2025, RC 6) — a switch from her March 2024 NO; (3) I0 Iran War Powers Resolution YES 2025-26 (with progressive motive but rubric-aligned restraint vote); (4) J4 NO FY24 NDAA + NO FY26 Defense Approps citing wasteful Pentagon spending (rubric-aligned defense-contractor accountability vote, partisan motive). AIPAC tracker shows $2.7M associated with AL-07. Religion: Brown Chapel AME Selma — cultural Christian via Black church tradition, but votes consistently against Christian-conservative rubric.

0.note: Opposed AL Human Life Protection Act and Dobbs; no on-record affirmation of personhood-from-conception.

1.note: NO Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act HR 26 (Jan 2023); cosponsor + YES Women's Health Protection Act HR 3755 (codifies Roe; nullifies state pro-life laws).

4.note: Planned Parenthood Action Fund 2026 endorsement; EMILY's List 'helped-elect' roster; Reproductive Freedom for All (NARAL successor) scorecard 100. Has accepted abortion-industry support.

0.note: Statement: 'No American should be discriminated against because of who they love' — affirms same-sex marriage rights.

1.note: YES Respect for Marriage Act HR 8404 (July 2022 RC 373 + Dec 8 2022 RC 513); only AL House member to support codifying SSM.

2.note: NO HR 734 Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act (Apr 2023, RC 192); all D caucus opposed.

4.note: YES Equality Act HR 5 (Feb 25, 2021, RC 39); member Congressional Equality Caucus; HRC 'Your Elected Officials' pro-equality listing.

0.note: NO Scholarships for Opportunity and Results Act (DC opportunity scholarships); no documented support for federal school choice.

1.note: NO Parents Bill of Rights HR 5 (Mar 24, 2023, RC 161, 213-208 with all voting Ds opposed party-line).

0.note: Party-line NO on HR 5403 CBDC Anti-Surveillance State Act (May 23, 2024, 216-192 with only 3 D defectors); Heritage Action 119th 0% reflects consistent D-caucus economic posture.

1.note: NO SAVE Act HR 8281; spoke from House floor in opposition; offered motion to recommit; calls SAVE Act 'voter suppression.'

2.note: Lead sponsor of John R. Lewis Voting Rights Advancement Act (expands early/mail voting); opposed to single-day-in-person framework.

3.note: YES Freedom to Vote: John R. Lewis Act (2022) — expands mail voting and drop-box access.

0.note: NO HR 2 Secure the Border Act (May 11, 2023, RC 209; passed with zero D support).

1.note: YES Dream/Promise Act 2021 (legalization for DACA-eligible); opposes mandatory-deportation framework.

3.note: CROSS-PARTY: Voted YES Farm Workforce Modernization Act 2021 — included nationwide mandatory E-Verify in exchange for H-2A reform; rare D vote backing E-Verify mandate.

0.note: NRA-PVF F grade — HARD evidence of opposition to 2A expansion incl. constitutional carry.

1.note: YES Bipartisan Safer Communities Act 2022 (red-flag funding); YES Bipartisan Background Checks 2019; YES reinstate Assault Weapons Ban.

2.note: NRA F grade; voting record consistent with maintaining/expanding NFA-GCA framework, not repealing it.

0.note: CROSS-PARTY: Cosponsor + YES Iran War Powers Resolution 2025; YES H.Con.Res.38 (2026) terminating unauthorized Iran hostilities. Article-I-restraint-aligned vote (motive partisan re: Trump but recorded vote rubric-aligned).

3.note: AIPAC tracker reports $2.7M pro-Israel-lobby money associated with AL-07; documented AIPAC-aligned among CBC centrists.

profile.religion: Southern Baptist — Central Baptist Church, Jonesboro, AR
profile.background: U.S. Representative AR-1 (R), in office since Jan 2011. Chairman, House Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence (named by Speaker Johnson Jan 16, 2025). Also House Agriculture Committee (senior). Univ. of Central Arkansas BA Ag Business. U.S. Army EOD veteran (Bronze Star Mtn Soldier). Founder, AgWatch radio network. Married Stacy; three children. AR GOP primary March 2026 canceled (no challenger); advances vs Dem Terri Yarbrough Green and Lib Steve Parsons in Nov 2026 general.

rick-crawford.notes_set: U.S. Rep AR-1 (R), in office since 2011; House Intelligence Chair since Jan 2025; House Agriculture senior member. [2026-06 federal autopilot] Heritage Action lifetime 70% / 119th 74% (volatile: 58% in 113th, 60% in 118th, 94% in 117th); NRA-PVF A; SBA Pro-Life endorsed/listed; FRC 92% (117th). Hard-evidence wins: YES Born-Alive HR 26; NO Respect for Marriage Act (party-line, all 4 AR Rs); YES HR 734 Women's Sports (party-line); NO Equality Act; YES OBBBA (folded in ECCA July 2025); YES Parents Bill of Rights HR 5 (not in defectors); cosponsor + YES HR 5403 CBDC Anti-Surveillance State Act; co-led BBA coalition (fiscal-cliff era); cosponsor + YES SAVE Act; YES HR 2 + Laken Riley Act + S.5; sponsor American Tech Workforce Act 2021 (mandatory E-Verify component); lead sponsor Agricultural Foreign Investment Transparency Act + PASS Act + Agricultural Intelligence Measures Act (foreign-adversary farmland); voted AGAINST $60.8B Ukraine aid bill April 2024 (restraint vote, citation in record). DEVIATIONS (R-from-conservative): (1) FISA Section 702 reauthorization — actively championed as Intel Chair, no warrant requirement — cross-checked against anti-surveillance Massie/Roy/Jordan wing; (2) Iran war-powers (2026) — as Intel Chair working to defeat Article-I-restraint resolutions on Trump's Iran action; (3) Big Ag donor capture — top industries crop production, agribusiness, dairy (Ag Committee insider). Religion: Southern Baptist, Central Baptist Jonesboro — culturally and policy-aligned with rubric.

4.note: Voted AGAINST $60.8B Ukraine aid supplemental April 2024 — restraint-aligned vote opposing NATO expansion / forever-war funding.

profile.background: U.S. Representative AR-2 (R), in office since Jan 2015. Chairman, House Financial Services Committee (elected by Steering Committee Dec 2024; took gavel Jan 3, 2025) — first former banker to chair HFSC in a century. Founder/CEO Delta Trust & Banking Corp. (Little Rock). Deputy Asst. Secretary of Treasury for Corporate Finance under GHW Bush (1989-91); Executive Secretary to Bush Economic Policy Council (1991). Vanderbilt BA Econ. Married Martha McKenzie (1988); two children. Won AR-2 GOP primary March 3, 2026 ~82% over Chase McDowell; faces Dem Chris Jones in Nov 2026 general.

french-hill.notes_set: U.S. Rep AR-2 (R), in office since 2015; House Financial Services Chair since Jan 2025; former banker (Delta Trust) + Treasury alum. [2026-06 federal autopilot] Heritage Action lifetime 70% / 116th 77%; FRC Action 100% (115th, 118th); NRA-PVF lifetime career top House recipient (~$1.09M); SBA Pro-Life listed; NRLC 100% pro-life. Hard-evidence wins: cosponsor Pain-Capable Unborn Child Protection Act; YES Born-Alive HR 26 (2023) + HR 21 (2025); NO Respect for Marriage Act HR 8404 BOTH votes; NO Equality Act; party-line YES OBBBA (ECCA); party-line YES Parents Bill of Rights HR 5; cosponsor + leader on HR 5403 CBDC Anti-Surveillance Act (HFSC Chair) + HR 1919 (119th passed July 2025); cosponsor multiple BBA resolutions; led HFSC ESG Working Group (anti-ESG agenda); cosponsor + supports SAVE Act; YES HR 2 + Laken Riley Act; cosponsor Constitutional Concealed Carry Reciprocity HR 38; NO BSCA 2022 + NO Bipartisan Background Checks. MAJOR DEVIATIONS (R-from-conservative): (1) UKRAINE AID — one of most vocally pro-Ukraine-aid House Rs; multiple press releases (Nov 2023 'U.S. MUST DRAW A LINE'); Zelenskyy visit Feb 2024; Heritage Action keyed Ukraine supplemental as NO and Hill went against (presumed YES on HR 8035); (2) J6 COMMISSION YES — one of only 35 Rs joining all Ds to establish independent Jan 6 commission (May 2021); (3) FED INDEPENDENCE — banker, no cosponsorship of HR 24 Audit-the-Fed; (4) FINANCE-INDUSTRY DONOR CAPTURE — Securities & Investment #1 industry ($839K 2024 cycle), BNY Mellon top contributor; (5) pro-Israel orientation, spoke at AIPAC conference. Religion: Catholic.

1.note: Cosponsor Pain-Capable Unborn Child Protection Act (2017, re-cosponsored 2022); YES Born-Alive HR 26 (2023, RC 29) and HR 21 (2025, RC 27); NRLC 100% pro-life.

profile.religion: Southern Baptist — Cross Church Pinnacle Hills, Rogers, AR
profile.background: U.S. Representative AR-3 (R), in office since Jan 2011. Senior member, House Appropriations Committee — Chairman, Transportation/HUD Subcommittee (re-appointed Jan 2025); also Defense Approps and Financial Services & General Government subs. Former Mayor of Rogers, AR (1998-2011). Arkansas Army National Guard, Colonel ret. (30+ years). Married Terri; three children. Filed for 9th term Nov 5, 2025; 2026 AR GOP primary canceled (uncontested); advances to Nov general.

steve-womack.notes_set: U.S. Rep AR-3 (R), in office since 2011; senior appropriator (Chair THUD sub); former Rogers mayor + AR Guard colonel. [2026-06 federal autopilot] Heritage Action 119th 74% / lifetime 66% (volatile: 117th 85%, 116th 77%, 114th only 55%); NRA-PVF endorsed (letter grade not surfaced). Hard-evidence wins: cosponsor Life at Conception Act HR 431; cosponsor Pain-Capable Act (115/116/117); YES Born-Alive HR 26 (RC 29); NO Respect for Marriage Act; NO Equality Act 2019 + 2021; YES Parents Bill of Rights HR 5; cosponsor + YES HR 5403 CBDC Anti-Surveillance State Act; YES SAVE Act HR 22 (Apr 10, 2025 RC 102); YES HR 2 + YES Laken Riley Act (Jan 2025); on-record sanctuary-city opposition; NO 2019 Bipartisan Background Checks Act; on-record 2022 Dem-gun-package opposition. MAJOR DEVIATIONS (R-from-conservative): (1) UKRAINE AID YES on HR 8035 supplemental Apr 20 2024 (RC 151) — 'staunch supporter'; (2) MCCARTHY OUSTER — presided over the vote, called motion-to-vacate 'a fool's errand'/'stain on the institution' — anti-Freedom-Caucus institutionalist; (3) YEMEN WAR POWERS — voted NAY on restraint resolutions per OnTheIssues; (4) BIG AG / TYSON CAPTURE — added GIPSA-blocking rider on Ag approps protecting Tyson; co-chairs Chicken Caucus; documented small-grower opposition; (5) AIPAC: $134,574 lifetime from pro-Israel PACs per TrackAIPAC, $98K in 2023-24 cycle alone; (6) APPROPRIATIONS-REALIST — YES on Sept 2024 CR despite BBA rhetoric. Religion: Southern Baptist (Cross Church Pinnacle Hills) — cultural/policy-aligned on life/marriage; deviates sharply on FP-restraint, Big Ag, fiscal vehicles.
